master_row.title: Manifold Curvature (TZP Condition)
master_row.canonical_statement: The Trawinistic scalar curvature $\TrawinCurv$ is defined by \( R_T = \lim_{x\to p}(R+f(x)) \), where $R$ is the usual Ricci scalar and $f(x)$ is a regulator function that cancels divergences such that $\TrawinCurv$ remains finite if and only if the lemniscatic self-intersection condition holds.
master_row.formation_inputs: Γ, Λ, x, p, R, f
master_row.formation_note: Documented in the source as a limiting process relating Γ, Λ, x, p, R, f.
master_row.dictionary: The Trawinistic scalar curvature (R_T) is defined by (R_T=_x p (R + f(x))), where (R) is the usual Ricci scalar and (f(x)) is a regulator function that cancels divergences such that (R_T) remains finite if and only if the lemniscatic self‑intersection condition holds.
master_row.encyclopedia: $R_T$ is the curvature seen through the TZP regulator. It separates uncontrolled singular behavior from a finite Trawinistic curvature condition. Interpretive role: This curvature definition lets the registry speak about geometric bending without surrendering the zero point to uncontrolled divergence. It also becomes the curvature input for coherence, wave, and connection operators built later in the sequence.
